heading: Regional innovation program
label: 3722 Regional innovation program
notes: Amendments2019— amended section generally. Prior to amendment, section related to regional innovation program, consisting of subsecs. (a) to (g).Pub. L. 116–922014— amended section generally. Prior to amendment, text related to regional innovation program and consisted of subsecs. (a) to (i), including provisions relating to establishment of program, cluster grants, science and research park development grants, loan guarantees for science park infrastructure, regional innovation research and information program, interagency coordination, evaluation of program, definitions, and authorization of appropriations.Pub. L. 113–235

(a) to (i), including provisions relating to establishment of program, cluster grants, science and research park development grants, loan guarantees for science park infrastructure, regional innovation research and information program, interagency coordination, evaluation of program, definitions, and authorization of appropriations.Pub. L. 113–235

children.label: A
children.text: a State;

children.label: B
children.text: an Indian tribe;

children.label: C
children.text: a city or other political subdivision of a State;

children.label: i
children.text: is a nonprofit organization, an institution of higher education, a public-private partnership, a science or research park, a Federal laboratory, a venture development organization, or an economic development organization or similar entity that is focused primarily on improving science, technology, innovation, or entrepreneurship; and

children.label: ii
children.text: has an application submitted under subsection (c)(4) that is supported by a State or a political subdivision of a State; or

children.label: E
children.text: a consortium of any of the entities described in subparagraphs (A) through (D).

children.label: A
children.text: increase the success of innovation-driven industry;

children.label: B
children.text: strengthen the competitiveness of industry through new product innovation and new technology adoption;

children.label: C
children.text: improve the pace of market readiness and overall commercialization of innovative research;

children.label: D
children.text: enhance the overall innovation capacity and long-term resilience of the region;

children.label: E
children.text: leverage the region’s unique competitive strengths to stimulate innovation; and

children.label: F
children.text: increase the number of full-time equivalent employment opportunities within innovation-based business ventures in the geographic region.

children.heading: State
children.label: 3 State
children.text: The term “State” means one of the several States of the United States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the United States Virgin Islands, Guam, American Samoa, the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands, or any other territory or possession of the United States.

children.heading: Venture development organization
children.label: 4 Venture development organization
children.text: The term “venture development organization” means a State or nonprofit organization that contributes to regional or sector-based economic prosperity by providing services for the purposes of accelerating the commercialization of research.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: b Establishment
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a regional innovation program to encourage and support the development of regional innovation strategies designed to increase innovation-driven economic opportunity within their respective regions.

children.heading: Authorization of grants
children.label: 1 Authorization of grants
children.text: As part of the program established pursuant to subsection (b), the Secretary may award grants, on a competitive basis, to eligible recipients for activities designed to develop and support a regional innovation initiative.

children.label: A
children.text: planning, technical assistance, and communication among participants of a regional innovation initiative to improve the connectedness and strategic orientation of the regional innovation initiative;

children.label: B
children.text: attracting additional participants to a regional innovation initiative;

children.label: C
children.text: increasing the availability and investment of private and philanthropic financing that supports innovation-based business ventures; and

children.label: D
children.text: facilitating commercialization of products, processes, and services, including through demonstration, deployment, technology transfer, and entrepreneurial activities.

children.label: A
children.text: costs related to the recruitment, inducement, or associated financial or tangible incentives that might be offered to relocate an existing business from a geographic area to another geographic area; or

children.label: B
children.text: costs associated with offsetting revenues forgone by 1 or more taxing authorities through tax incentives, tax increment financing, special improvement districts, tax abatements for private development within designated zones or geographic areas, or other reduction in revenues resulting from tax credits affecting the geographic region of the eligible recipients.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: An eligible recipient shall submit an application to the Secretary at such time, in such manner, and containing such information and assurances as the Secretary may require.

children.label: i
children.text: describe the regional innovation initiative;

children.label: ii
children.text: indicate whether the regional innovation initiative is supported by the private sector, State and local governments, and other relevant stakeholders;

children.label: iii
children.text: identify what activities the regional innovation initiative will undertake;

children.label: iv
children.text: describe the expected outcomes of the regional innovation initiative and the metrics the eligible recipient will use to assess progress toward those outcomes;

children.label: v
children.text: indicate whether the participants in the regional innovation initiative have access to, or contribute to, a well-trained workforce and other innovation assets that are critical to the successful outcomes specified in the application;

children.label: vi
children.text: indicate whether the participants in the regional innovation initiative are capable of attracting additional funds from non-Federal sources; and

children.label: vii
children.text: if appropriate for the activities proposed in the application, analyze the likelihood that the participants in the regional innovation initiative will be able to sustain activities after grant funds received under this subsection have been expended.

children.heading: Feedback
children.label: C Feedback
children.text: The Secretary shall provide feedback to program applicants that are not awarded grants to help them improve future applications.

children.label: i
children.text: applications proposing to include workforce or training related activities in their regional innovation initiative from eligible recipients who agree to collaborate with local workforce investment area boards; and

children.label: ii
children.text: applications from regions that contain communities negatively impacted by trade.

children.heading: Cost share
children.label: 5 Cost share
children.text: The Secretary may not provide more than 50 percent of the total cost of any activity funded under this subsection.

children.heading: Outreach to rural communities
children.label: 6 Outreach to rural communities
children.text: The Secretary shall conduct outreach to public and private sector entities in rural communities to encourage those entities to participate in regional innovation initiatives under this subsection.

children.heading: Geographic distribution
children.label: 7 Geographic distribution
children.text: In conducting a competitive process, the Secretary shall avoid undue geographic concentration among any one category of States based on their predominant rural or urban character as indicated by population density.

children.heading: Funding
children.label: 8 Funding
children.text: The Secretary may accept funds from other Federal agencies to support grants and activities under this subsection.

children.heading: Regional innovation research and information program
children.label: d Regional innovation research and information program
A
children.text: to gather, analyze, and disseminate information on best practices for regional innovation initiatives, including information relating to how innovation, productivity, and economic development can be maximized through such strategies;

children.label: B
children.text: to provide technical assistance, including through the development of technical assistance guides, for the development and implementation of regional innovation initiatives;

children.label: C
children.text: to support the development of relevant metrics and measurement standards to evaluate regional innovation initiatives, including the extent to which such strategies stimulate innovation, productivity, and economic development; and

children.label: i
children.text: the size, specialization, and competitiveness of regional innovation initiatives;

children.label: ii
children.text: the regional domestic product contribution, total jobs and earnings by key occupations, establishment size, nature of specialization, patents, Federal research and development spending, and other relevant information for regional innovation initiatives; and

children.label: iii
children.text: supply chain product and service flows within and between regional innovation initiatives.

children.heading: Research grants
children.label: 2 Research grants
children.text: The Secretary may award research grants on a competitive basis to support and further the goals of the program established under this section.

children.heading: Dissemination of information
children.label: 3 Dissemination of information
children.text: Data and analysis compiled by the Secretary under the program established in this subsection shall be made available to other Federal agencies, State and local governments, and nonprofit and for-profit entities.

children.heading: Regional innovation grant program
children.label: 4 Regional innovation grant program
children.text: The Secretary shall incorporate data and analysis relating to any grant awarded under subsection (c) into the program established under this subsection.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: To the maximum extent practicable, the Secretary shall ensure that the activities carried out under this section are coordinated with, and do not duplicate the efforts of, other programs at the Department of Commerce or at other Federal agencies.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall explore and pursue collaboration with other Federal agencies, including through multi-agency funding opportunities, on regional innovation strategies.

children.heading: Small businesses
children.label: B Small businesses
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that such collaboration with Federal agencies prioritizes the needs and challenges of small businesses.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 5 years after Congress first appropriates funds to carry out this section, the Secretary shall competitively award a contract with an independent entity to conduct an evaluation of programs established under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: an assessment of whether the program is achieving its goals;

children.label: B
children.text: the program’s efficacy in providing awards to geographically diverse entities;

children.label: C
children.text: any recommendations for how the program may be improved; and

children.label: D
children.text: a recommendation as to whether the program should be continued or terminated.

children.heading: Reporting requirement
children.label: g Reporting requirement
children.text: Not later than 5 years after the first grant is awarded under subsection (c), and every 5 years thereafter until 5 years after the last grant recipient completes the regional innovation initiative for which such grant was awarded, the Secretary shall submit a summary report to Congress that describes the outcome of each regional innovation initiative that was completed during the previous 5 years.

children.heading: Funding
children.label: h Funding
children.text: From amounts appropriated by Congress to the Secretary, the Secretary may use up to $50,000,000 in each of the fiscal years 2020 through 2024 to carry out this section.
